[號]
Originally an ideogram ( 指事 ) of a tiger with a line representing exhaled air while roaring, thus meaning "to roar; to shout". Later forms added 口 (“mouth”) or 言 (“speech”) to emphasize the act of crying or calling. 号 derives from 口 and the line 丂 .
In the current form, an ideogrammic compound ( 會意 / 会意 ) and phono-semantic compound ( 形聲 / 形声 , OC *ɦlaːw, *ɦlaːws ): phonetic 号 ( OC *ɦlaːws , “ to wail ” ) + semantic 虎 ( “ tiger ” ) .

[号]
Ideogrammic compound ( 會意 / 会意 ): 口 ( “ mouth ” ) + 丂 ( “ exhaled air; a tiger's cry ” ) . See 號 for more details.
